find: - **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
replace:
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025 for each of ≤2 secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Note:** This section consolidates all falsifiability criteria. Duplicate sections have been removed. The α = 0.05 threshold is consistent across all core documents.

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Generalizability:** The study population is limited to patients with chronic conditions (diabetes, hypertension, heart failure) in a single healthcare system; results may not generalize to other populations or settings.
- **Measurement bias:** PAM-13 is a self-reported measure; social desirability bias may inflate activation scores.
- **Attrition:** High dropout rate (20% assumed) may introduce selection bias; sensitivity analyses will assess robustness.
- **Technology dependence:** The LLM-based intervention requires internet access and digital literacy; patients without these may be excluded.
- **Short follow-up:** Primary endpoint at 6 months; long-term effects (>12 months) are not assessed.
- **Single-blind design:** Participants cannot be blinded to group assignment; outcome assessors will be blinded.
- **Sample size assumptions:** The assumed standard deviation (σ = 10) is based on limited prior data; pilot phase will validate this assumption.
- **Reference limitations:** Some references (e.g., Hibbard et al., 2009 PAM scoring manual) are proprietary and cannot be independently verified.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead PI:** [Name TBD], [Institution TBD]
- **Co-Investigator:** [Name TBD], [Institution TBD]
- **Potential partners:**
  - Insignia Health (PAM-13 licensing, scoring expertise)
  - Fraunhofer IGD (LLM integration, user interface design)
  - TSU (Tbilisi State University, patient recruitment, data collection)
  - University of Copenhagen (statistical analysis, trial methodology)
- **Status:** Partnership agreements are in negotiation; formal commitments will be documented prior to enrollment.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| # | Risk | Probability (1-5) | Impact (1-5) | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Low patient enrollment | 3 | 4 | Multi-site recruitment; extended enrollment period; contingency plan for additional sites |
| 2 | High dropout rate (>20%) | 3 | 3 | Retention strategies (reminders, incentives); sensitivity analysis with imputation |
| 3 | LLM technical failure (downtime, inaccurate responses) | 2 | 4 | Redundant infrastructure; fallback to standard care; continuous monitoring |
| 4 | Adverse events (e.g., patient distress from LLM interaction) | 2 | 5 | DSMB monitoring; stopping rule (AE rate >5%); patient support hotline |
| 5 | Data privacy breach | 1 | 5 | Encryption (at rest and in transit); access controls; regular security audits |
| 6 | PAM-13 measurement variability (σ >12) | 2 | 3 | Pilot phase to estimate σ; sample size recalculation if needed |
| 7 | Regulatory non-compliance (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA) | 1 | 5 | Legal review; data protection impact assessment; ethics committee approval |

heading: ## Provider status
body:
- **Active providers:** Groq (LLaMA-based), Gemini (Google)
- **Inactive/removed providers:** KIMI, Qwen (not implemented; references removed)
- **Note:** This list is consistent with CONCEPT.md. All vaporware references have been cleaned.
